title: • Example Transformations:
content:
• Example Transformations:
• Dr. Jekyll & Mr. Hyde – Gains deception & power buffs but must roll for control each turn.
• Odysseus (The Survivor) – Always escapes combat but loses group-based abilities.
• The Invisible Man – Cannot be targeted but can only act through influence, not direct action.

3️⃣ Chaotic (Corruptive, World-Altering Paths – “The Fall of Order”)
• Villain Evolution (Becoming a story-changing antagonist) → Only available to Tricksters, Wanderers, & Exiles.
• Example Transformations:
• Lucifer (The Ultimate Betrayer) – Gains charisma-based advantages but is permanently exiled from factions.
• Iago (The Manipulator) – Can shift faction decisions but must work in secrecy.
• Frankenstein’s Monster (The Rejected Creation) – Gains extreme power but can no longer form alliances.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Encourages students to consider long-term evolution.
✔ Gives both heroes and villains distinct pathways.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 491 (chunk 491/555)
